bootstrap.title: GroupMixer solver contracts
bootstrap.summary: Static bootstrap for the public solver affordance graph shared by CLI, HTTP, and WASM projections.
bootstrap.discovery_note: The surface is static and self-describing. Start from a top-level operation, then request local help for that operation and follow its related affordances.

operations.summary: Run the solver for a complete optimization input.
operations.description: Accept a full solver input, execute the optimization engine, and return the resulting schedule plus final metrics.

operations.summary: Validate solver input without running optimization.
operations.description: Check whether a solver input is structurally and semantically acceptable before invoking the solver.

operations.summary: Inspect lightweight metadata for an existing solver result.
operations.description: Return summary-level result fields that are useful for follow-up inspection and discovery without requiring a full schedule walk.

operations.summary: Recommend solver settings from a problem definition.
operations.description: Analyze a problem definition and return a recommended solver configuration without executing the main solve workflow.

operations.summary: Evaluate an existing scheduled input without running search.
operations.description: Accept a solve request that already includes an initial schedule, recompute scores, and return the resulting solver result payload.

operations.summary: Inspect a named public schema from the contract registry.
operations.description: Return machine-readable schema metadata for one stable schema identifier.

operations.summary: Inspect the canonical public error catalog.
operations.description: Return the stable error-code catalog so callers can understand failure meanings and follow related-help pointers.

errors.summary: The provided request or payload shape is invalid.
errors.why: The caller supplied malformed JSON, a missing required field, or an unexpected value shape for the requested operation.
errors.recovery: Inspect the relevant input schema and local help for the targeted operation, then resend a valid request.

errors.summary: The requested operation is not part of the public solver contract.
errors.why: The caller referenced an operation ID or affordance name that is not registered in the shared contracts graph.
errors.recovery: Start from bootstrap help, then choose one of the registered top-level operations.

errors.summary: The requested schema ID is not registered.
errors.why: The caller requested a schema identifier that does not exist in the contract registry.
errors.recovery: Inspect the schema lookup/help affordance and request one of the registered schema IDs.

errors.summary: The requested public error code is not registered.
errors.why: The caller requested an error code identifier that does not exist in the contract registry.
errors.recovery: Inspect the error-catalog help affordance and request one of the registered public error codes.

errors.summary: The problem definition is internally inconsistent or infeasible.
errors.why: The solver cannot satisfy the required constraints, capacities, or session assignments for the provided input.
errors.recovery: Validate the problem, inspect constraint settings, and adjust the input so a valid schedule is possible.

errors.summary: A constraint kind or similar enum-like value is not supported.
errors.why: The caller referenced a named domain feature that is not part of the currently supported public contract.
errors.recovery: Inspect the relevant schema and contract help, then replace the unsupported value with a supported one.

errors.summary: The caller lacks permission for the requested operation.
errors.why: The current transport/runtime surface rejected the action under its active rights model.
errors.recovery: Use a permitted read-only affordance, obtain the required credentials/scope, or switch to an allowed workflow.

errors.why: The operation failed for a reason outside the normal public validation and feasibility model.
errors.recovery: Capture the error details, inspect local help for the attempted operation, and retry only if the failure is known to be transient.

examples.description: Shows the smallest complete optimization input and a representative successful result plus transport-specific invocation snippets.

snippets.label: http invocation
snippets.content: POST /solve with the solve request JSON body

examples.summary: Validation failure for an unsupported constraint kind.
examples.description: Demonstrates a negative path where the caller gets a precise validation issue and recovery pointers.

snippets.label: cli recovery
snippets.content: solver-cli validate input.json && solver-cli schema input

examples.description: Shows the compact result metadata shape used by result-inspection affordances.
Shows the structured public error envelope shape shared across projections.

snippets.label: public error envelope
snippets.content:
{
  "error": {
    "code": "unsupported-constraint-kind",
    "message": "Constraint kind 'ShouldBeTogether' is not supported.",
    "where_path": "constraints[0].type",
    "why": "The caller referenced a constraint type outside the supported public contract.",
    "valid_alternatives": [
      "RepeatEncounter",
      "AttributeBalance",
      "MustStayTogether",
      "ShouldStayTogether",
      "ShouldNotBeTogether",
      "ImmovablePerson",
      "ImmovablePeople",
      "PairMeetingCount"
    ],
    "recovery": "Inspect the relevant schema/help and replace the unsupported constraint kind.",
    "related_help": ["validate-problem", "get-schema"]
  }
}

examples.description: Shows a transport-specific invocation that targets the solve-request schema.

examples.summary: Recommend solver settings from a problem definition.
examples.description: Shows a minimal problem definition and a representative recommended solver configuration.

examples.summary: Evaluate a scheduled input without running search.
examples.description: Shows the shape of a representative evaluation result for an input that already includes an initial schedule.
